privileged_info_summary: The task requires the agent to configure Stellarium using real-world coordinates for Skukuza, South Africa (-24.99°, 31.59°) and track the Moon's ephemeris on September 18, 2024. All metadata claims exactly match the explicit instructions provided in the task description. However, a critical bug exists in the verifier.py logic: when evaluating the poaching patrol plan text file, it strictly only checks for the presence of the keywords 'rise', 'transit', and 'set' alongside any three generic 'HH:MM' formatted time strings. The verifier completely fails to check if the reported times are astronomically correct (the true UTC times are ~16:21, 22:31, and 04:33). Furthermore, the export scripts and verifier entirely neglect to check the target altitude (265m), target date, and 'flag_planet_labels' setting, granting full credit for these criteria regardless of the agent's actions as long as other display toggles and location coordinates are met.
